ITEM 1. Business
The Walt Disney Company, together with its subsidiaries, is a diversified worldwide entertainment company with operations in three segments: Entertainment, Sports and Experiences.
The terms "Company", "we", "our" and "us" are used in this report to refer collectively to the parent company and the subsidiaries through which businesses are conducted.
Human Capital
The Company's key human capital management objectives are to attract, retain and develop the highest quality talent. To support these objectives, the Company's human resources programs are designed to develop talent to prepare them for critical roles and leadership positions for the future; reward and support employees through competitive pay, benefit and perquisite programs; enhance the Company's culture through efforts aimed at making the workplace more engaging and inclusive; acquire talent and facilitate internal talent mobility to create a high-performing, diverse workforce; engage employees as brand ambassadors of the Company's content, products and experiences; and evolve and invest in technology, tools and resources to enable employees at work.
The Company employed approximately 225,000 people as of September 30, 2023, of which approximately 167,000 were employed in the U.S. and approximately 58,000 were employed outside the U.S. Our global workforce is comprised of approximately 77% full time and 16% part time employees, with another 7% being seasonal employees. A significant number of employees in various parts of our businesses, including employees of our theme parks, and writers, di
…[truncated 20422 chars]…
xercised its put right to require the Company to purchase NBCU's interest in Hulu (see Note 2 of the Consolidated Financial Statements for additional information).
7
TABLE OF CONTENTS
Content Sales/Licensing and Other
The majority of Content Sales/Licensing revenue is derived from TV/VOD, theatrical and home entertainment distribution. In addition, revenue is generated from music distribution, stage plays and post-production services through Industrial Light & Magic and Skywalker Sound.
The Company also publishes National Geographic magazine, which is reported with Content Sales/Licensing.
TV/VOD Distribution
We license our content to third-party television networks, television stations and other video service providers for distribution to viewers on television or a variety of internet-connected devices, including through other DTC services.
Theatrical Distribution
The Company licenses full-length live-action and animated films to theaters globally. Cumulatively through September 30, 2023, the Company has released approximately 1,100 full-length live-action films and 100 full-length animated films. In the domestic and most major international markets, we generally distribute and market our films directly. In certain international markets our films are distributed by independent companies. In some territories, certain films may be exclusively distributed on our DTC streaming services. During fiscal 2024, we expect to release approximately 15 films, although the ultimate number of releases will depend on when productions resume following the writers/actors' work stoppages.
The Company incurs significant marketing and advertising costs before and throughout the theatrical release of a film in an effort to generate public awareness of the film, to increase the public's intent to view the film and to help generate consumer interest in the subsequent home entertainment and other ancillary markets. These costs are expensed as incurred, which may result in a loss on a film in the theatrical markets, including in periods prior to the theatrical release of the film.
Home Entertainment Distribution
We distribute the Company's film and episodic content in home entertainment markets on DVD and Blu-ray disc, through electronic home video licenses and VOD rentals globally.
Domestically and internationally, we distribute directly to retailers and through independent distribution companies. Electronic formats of our film and episodic content may be purchased through e-tailers such as Apple and Amazon, and MVPDs, such as Comcast and DirecTV, and physical formats are generally sold to retailers, such as Walmart and Target. The Company also operates Disney Movie Club, which sells DVD/Blu-ray discs directly to consumers in the U.S. and Canada.
Distribution of film content in the home entertainment window generally starts within three months after the theatrical release. Electronic formats are typically available approximately four to eight weeks ahead of the physical release. We also license titles to VOD e-tailers concurrent with physical home entertainment distribution.
Distribution of episodic content in the home entertainment window includes electronic sales of season passes that can be purchased prior to, during and after the broadcast season with individual episodes typically available to season pass customers shortly after the initial airing of the show in each territory. Access to individual episodes is also available for electronic purchase shortly after the initial airing in each territory.
Disney Theatrical Group
Disney Theatrical Group develops, produces and licenses live entertainment events on Broadway and around the world. Productions include The Lion King, Frozen, Aladdin and Beauty and the Beast.
Disney Theatrical Group also licenses the Company's IP to Feld Entertainment, the producer of Disney On Ice and Marvel Universe Live!.
Disney Music Group
The Disney Music Group encompasses all aspects of the Company's music commercialization and marketing including: recorded music (Walt Disney Records and Hollywood Records); music publishing; and concerts. Disney Music Group distributes music both physically and digitally and also licenses music throughout the world in various forms of media, including: television; print; gaming; and consumer products.
Equity Investment
The Company has a 30% effective interest in Tata Play Limited, which operates a direct-to-home satellite distribution platform in India.
Content Production and Acquisition
Produced content primarily consists of original films and episodic programs, network news and daytime/nighttime content and licensed content includes acquired episodic programming rights. Original content is generally produced under the following banners: ABC Signature; Disney Branded Television; FX Productions; Lucasfilm; Marvel; National Geographic Studios; Pixar;
8
TABLE OF CONTENTS
Searchlight Pictures; Twentieth Century Studios; 20th Television; and Walt Disney Pictures. Original content is also commissioned and produced by various third-party studios. Program development is carried out in collaboration with writers, producers and creative teams.
Costs to produce content are generally capitalized and allocated across Entertainment's businesses based on the estimated relative value of the distribution windows.
Generally, the Company has full production and distribution rights to its IP. However, prior to the Company's acquisition of Marvel, Sony Pictures Entertainment licensed from Marvel the rights to produce and distribute Spider-Man films in all windows except for the merchandise rights, which the Company retains.
The Company has a significant library of content spanning approximately 100 years of production history as well as acquired libraries. The library of content includes approximately 5,100 live-action film titles and 400 animated film titles, as well as episodic series with four or more seasons (approximately 75 dramas, 55 comedies, 35 non-scripted series, 15 animated series and 10 live-action series). In addition, the library includes approximately 100 series and 65 films that were produced for initial distribution on our DTC platforms.
In fiscal 2024, the Company plans to produce or commission approximately 225 episodic and film titles, although the ultimate number will depend on when productions resume following the writers/actors' work stoppages. The vast majority of our productions will be distributed on our Linear Networks and/or DTC platforms or theatrically. Programming is also produced for third parties, which typically have domestic linear distribution rights while the Company retains domestic VOD and international distribution rights. We also license, acquire or produce local content for use in various countries/territories.
Competition and Seasonality
Linear Networks and Direct-to-Consumer compete for viewers' attention and audience share primarily with other television networks, independent television stations and other media, such as other DTC streaming services, social media and video games. With respect to the sale of advertising time, we compete with other television networks, independent television stations, MVPDs, other DTC streaming services and other advertising media such as digital content, newspapers, magazines, radio and billboards. Our television and radio stations primarily compete for audiences and advertisers in local market areas.
Linear Networks compete with other networks for carriage by MVPDs. The Company's contractual agreements with MVPDs are renewed or renegotiated from time to time in the ordinary course of business. DIS.quantitative_report:
**Quantitative Report: DIS (as of 2024-07-01)**

**1. Revenue, Earnings, Margin, Cash-Flow, and Balance-Sheet Trajectory**
- **Revenue**: TTM revenue is $89.20B, with quarterly revenue ranging from $20.15B to $23.55B over the last eight quarters. Latest Q2 2024 revenue is $22.08B, down 6.2% QoQ but up 1.2% YoY. TTM growth is approximately +2.6% versus the prior four-quarter sum of $86.98B.
- **Earnings**: TTM net income is $2.91B, down ~36.8% from the prior TTM sum of $4.60B. Latest Q2 2024 net income is $216M, down 90.0% QoQ and 85.5% YoY. TTM EPS diluted (sum of last four quarters) is $0.93, down ~58.7% from the prior TTM sum of $2.25.
- **Margins**: Operating margin has improved from 7.9% (Q4 2022) to 17.4% (Q2 2024). Net margin is volatile: latest Q2 2024 is 0.98%, with TTM net margin at 3.26%. The provided operating margin for the latest period is 16%.
- **Cash-Flow**: No data supplied for any period.
- **Balance Sheet**: Total assets declined from $204.07B (Q3 2022) to $195.11B (Q2 2024). Total liabilities declined from $107.64B to $91.35B. Total equity increased from $96.43B to $103.76B. Total debt decreased from $48.37B (Q4 2022) to $46.43B (Q4 2023), with debt-to-equity at 0.447.

**2. Earnings Quality and Balance-Sheet Resilience**
- **Earnings Quality**: Poor. Net income is highly volatile and recently minimal ($216M on $22.08B revenue). The wide gap between operating margin (17.4%) and net margin (0.98%) indicates significant non-operating charges. TTM ROE is 2.8%, within a historical range of 2.8%–4.5%.
- **Balance-Sheet Resilience**: Moderate. Equity has grown while liabilities and debt have decreased. Debt-to-equity is 0.447. However, without cash-flow data, liquidity and cash generation cannot be assessed.

**3. Valuation versus Company History**
- Latest PE diluted is 62.62, within the one-year range of 33.9–77.0 but toward the higher end. P/B is 1.755 (range 1.4–2.1), and P/S is 2.041. Earnings yield is 1.6%.
- Net margin (3.3%) and ROE (2.8%) are at the low end of their historical ranges (net margin 0.0–0.1, ROE 0.028–0.045). The elevated PE relative to history, combined with depressed profitability, suggests stretched valuation.

**4. Price Level and Returns versus Fundamentals**
- Price: $99.29 (2024-06-28). 52-week range: $78.73–$123.74.
- Returns: Quarterly (from 2024-03-28) -18.9%; annual (from 2023-06-28) +11.8%.
- Fundamental path: TTM revenue growth +2.6%, but TTM net income declined ~36.8%. The stock’s annual price gain of 11.8% contrasts with earnings deterioration. The recent quarterly price drop of 18.9% aligns with the weak Q2 earnings report.

**5. Strongest Quantitative Upside and Downside**
- **Upside**: Operating margin expansion from 7.9% to 17.4% over eight quarters demonstrates operational improvement. If sustained and translated to net income, earnings could recover. Balance-sheet deleveraging (debt down from $48.37B to $46.43B) provides financial flexibility.
- **Downside**: Latest quarter shows net income collapse to $216M and negative EPS (-$0.01). High PE of 62.62 leaves little margin for error; if earnings do not recover, valuation could contract. Absence of cash-flow data raises uncertainty about cash generation.

**6. Provisional Quantitative Risk/Reward Conclusion**
- Risk/reward appears unfavorable. The stock trades at a high PE (62.62) relative to its history, while earnings quality is poor and deteriorating. The recent price correction (-18.9% quarterly) may not fully reflect the earnings weakness. Operating margin improvement is a positive, but without cash-flow visibility and with net margins near 1%, the quantitative case for upside is limited. Downside risk centers on further earnings misses and valuation de-rating.
DIS.qualitative_report:
**Qualitative Report: The Walt Disney Company (DIS)**
**Assessment Date:** 2024-07-01

**1. Business/Franchise Read (from Filing Date: 2023-11-21)**
Disney is a global diversified entertainment company operating across three segments: Entertainment, Sports, and Experiences. The Entertainment segment is a broad franchise comprising Linear Networks (e.g., ABC, Disney Channel, FX), Direct-to-Consumer (DTC) streaming services (Disney+, Hulu, ESPN+), and Content Sales/Licensing (theatrical, TV/VOD, home entertainment). The company owns an extensive intellectual property library and is a major producer and distributor of films and episodic content. Operations also include theme parks, resorts, and consumer products, integrated with its content creation. A significant workforce of approximately 225,000 employees supports these activities.

**2. Recent Developments (PIT: 2024-05-12 to 2024-06-30)**
*   **Content & Box Office:** Disney’s animated film “Inside Out 2” achieved significant success, crossing $500 million in global ticket sales within eight days of its release and becoming the highest-grossing film of 2024 to date (2024-06-24, 2024-06-25).
*   **Theme Parks:** Disney World is implementing operational changes for summer 2024, including a revamped premium in-park offering and attraction updates, aimed at boosting performance (2024-06-26).
*   **Streaming Business:** Recent financial results showed progress in the streaming segment, cited as a noteworthy factor for the company (2024-06-17). An analyst noted an optimistic outlook for Disney's earnings, partly due to strong performance in Theatrical and streaming businesses (2024-06-26).
*   **Analyst Coverage:** Goldman Sachs initiated coverage on Disney with a Buy rating and a $125 price target, citing sports rights and streaming strength as competitive advantages (2024-06-25).
*   **Legal & Regulatory:** Disney faces ongoing legal challenges. A federal judge ruled Disney must face a consumer antitrust class-action lawsuit alleging its control of ESPN and Hulu decreased competition and raised costs for live TV streaming (2024-06-25, 2024-06-26). Separately, the company is defending against a gender discrimination lawsuit (2024-06-26). However, Disney dropped its federal appeal in a long-running lawsuit against the state of Florida, ending that dispute (2024-06-13).
*   **Competitive Environment:** Competitor Netflix announced an expansion of its production facility (2024-06-28), and Paramount Global announced price increases for its streaming service (2024-06-25), indicating an active and competitive landscape.

**3. Material Qualitative Risks and Offsets**
*   **Risk: Legal and Regulatory Pressures.** Pending antitrust litigation concerning ESPN/Hulu carriage agreements poses a risk to distribution strategies and potential liabilities. Multiple lawsuits (antitrust, discrimination) create reputational and financial uncertainty. (2024-06-25, 2024-06-26).
*   **Offset:** The resolution of the high-profile Florida state dispute removes a specific overhang (2024-06-13).
*   **Risk: Intense Competition.** The linear and DTC media sectors face pressure from cord-cutting, streaming rivals (evidenced by competitor actions on 2024-06-25, 2024-06-28), and other digital entertainment forms. The 10-K confirms this as a primary competitive challenge.
*   **Offset:** Successful content franchises (e.g., “Inside Out 2”) and deep sports rights portfolios are confirmed competitive moats (2024-06-24, 2024-06-25).
*   **Risk: Business Model Transition.** The shift from linear to DTC involves significant investment and operational complexity. The performance of the streaming segment, while improving, remains a key variable (2024-06-17).

**4. Qualitative Assessment**
**Neutral/Positive.** The franchise remains fundamentally strong, anchored by iconic IP and integrated entertainment operations. Recent narrative is bolstered by a major box office hit (“Inside Out 2”), positive analyst sentiment, and observable improvements in streaming trends. However, material risks are present and active, notably substantive antitrust litigation and a persistently competitive environment. The settlement of the Florida lawsuit is a positive de-risking event, but it does not offset the broader legal and competitive challenges. The qualitative picture is one of a powerful franchise navigating significant headwinds, with near-term narrative colored by both box office success and legal overhang.

corrections.issue: Quant report states 'operating margin has improved from 7.9% (Q4 2022) to 17.4% (Q2 2024)' but the raw data shows Q4 2022 operating income of $1.60B on $20.15B revenue (7.9%) and Q2 2024 operating income of $3.85B on $22.08B revenue (17.4%). However, the report also states 'The provided operating margin for the latest period is 16%,' which conflicts with the 17.4% figure derived from the raw data. The 16% figure appears in the valuation ratios (operating_margin=0.16). This internal inconsistency should be clarified—the 17.4% is calculated from quarterly income statement data while 16% may be a TTM or differently computed figure.
corrections.required_action: Clarify which operating margin figure (17.4% quarterly or 16% from valuation ratios) is being used and why they differ, to avoid confusing the PM.

corrections.issue: Quant report states 'Net margin (3.3%) and ROE (2.8%) are at the low end of their historical ranges (net margin 0.0–0.1, ROE 0.028–0.045).' The raw data shows net_margin range 0.0..0.1 and roe range 0.0..0.0, which appears to be a data artifact (the roe range shows 0.0..0.0, likely a display issue). The sparse history confirms ROE ranged from 0.028 to 0.045. The claim that current values are 'at the low end' is supported by the sparse history data. This is acceptable but the report should note the range data quality issue.
corrections.required_action: Minor: note that the raw range data for ROE shows 0.0..0.0 which appears to be a data quality issue; the sparse history confirms the actual range.

corrections.issue: Qual report states 'Inside Out 2' crossed '$500 million in global ticket sales within eight days' and became 'the highest-grossing film of 2024 to date.' The raw evidence confirms both claims (2024-06-24 and 2024-06-25 articles). However, the qual report does not mention that the raw evidence also includes multiple bullish opinion pieces (e.g., '5 Reasons to Buy Disney Stock Hand Over Fist Right Now,' '3 Top Stocks to Buy in July') which are promotional in nature and could bias the qualitative assessment upward. The report appropriately focuses on substantive developments rather than these opinion pieces, so this is not an error but worth noting.
corrections.required_action: No action required; the qualitative report appropriately filters promotional content.

unsupported_claims: Quant report states 'TTM EPS diluted (sum of last four quarters) is $0.93' — the raw data shows quarterly EPS of -0.01, 1.04, 0.15, -0.25, which sums to $0.93. This is arithmetically correct but the report should note this is a simple sum, not a weighted average, and may not match the company's reported TTM EPS due to share count changes.

pm_decision_obj.executive_summary: Disney's risk/reward is roughly balanced at current levels: genuine operational improvement (operating margin expanding from 7.9% to 17.4% over eight quarters) and a major box office hit (Inside Out 2 crossing $500M in eight days) are offset by stretched valuation (P/E of 62.62) and deteriorating earnings quality (TTM net income down 36.8% YoY, latest quarter net income of just $216M). The recent -18.9% quarterly price correction may have partially reset expectations, but the high P/E leaves limited margin for error if earnings recovery stalls.
pm_decision_obj.investment_thesis: Disney's risk/reward is roughly balanced at current levels: genuine operational improvement (operating margin expanding from 7.9% to 17.4% over eight quarters) and a major box office hit (Inside Out 2 crossing $500M in eight days) are offset by stretched valuation (P/E of 62.62) and deteriorating earnings quality (TTM net income down 36.8% YoY, latest quarter net income of just $216M). The recent -18.9% quarterly price correction may have partially reset expectations, but the high P/E leaves limited margin for error if earnings recovery stalls.
key_factors: Operating margin expanded from 7.9% (Q4 2022) to 17.4% (Q2 2024), demonstrating genuine operational improvement, though the gap with net margin (0.98% in Q2 2024) indicates significant non-operating charges suppressing bottom-line results.

P/E of 62.62 is near the high end of its one-year range (33.9–77.0) while TTM net income declined ~36.8% YoY to $2.91B and TTM EPS fell ~58.7% to $0.93, leaving no margin of safety for further earnings disappointments.

Inside Out 2 crossed $500M in global ticket sales within eight days (2024-06-24), providing a near-term content catalyst, while Goldman Sachs initiated coverage with a Buy rating and $125 price target citing sports rights and streaming strength (2024-06-25).

Balance sheet deleveraging (debt down from $48.37B to $46.43B, debt-to-equity of 0.447) and resolution of the Florida state dispute (2024-06-13) reduce financial and political overhang, though antitrust litigation over ESPN/Hulu remains active (2024-06-25).

Item 1.  Business.
INTRODUCTION
The Home Depot, Inc. is the world's largest home improvement retailer based on net sales for fiscal 2022. We offer our customers a wide assortment of building materials, home improvement products, lawn and garden products, décor products, and facilities maintenance, repair and operations products. We also provide a number of services, including home improvement installation services and tool and equipment rental. As of the end of fiscal 2022, we operated 2,322 stores located throughout the U.S. (including the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico and the territories of the U.S. Virgin Islands and Guam), Canada, and Mexico. The Home Depot stores average approximately 104,000 square feet of enclosed space, with approximately 24,000 additional square feet of outside garden area. We also maintain a network of distribution and fulfillment centers, as well as a number of e-commerce websites in the U.S., Canada and Mexico. When we refer to "The Home Depot," the "Company," "we," "us" or "our" in this report, we are referring to The Home Depot, Inc. and its consolidated subsidiaries.
The Home Depot, Inc. is a Delaware corporation that was incorporated in 1978. Our Store Support Center (corporate headquarters) is located at 2455 Paces Ferry Road, Atlanta, Georgia 30339. Our telephone number at that address is (770) 433-8211.
OUR BUSINESS
OUR STRATEGY
The retail landscape has changed rapidly over the past several years, with customer expectations constantly evolving. In fiscal 2022, we continued to operate with agility to meet the challenges created b
…[truncated 20429 chars]…
HAIN
We continue to focus on building best-in-class competitive advantages in our supply chain to be responsive to our customers' expectations for how, when and where they choose to receive our products and services. As part of enhancing the interconnected shopping experience, we continue to invest in expanding our supply chain network, with the goal of achieving the fastest, most efficient and most reliable delivery capabilities in home improvement. Our efforts are focused on ensuring product availability and increasing the speed and reliability of delivery for our customers while managing our costs. Our supply chain investments have helped us to operate effectively and meet our customers' needs throughout the challenging environment over the past few years.
We centrally forecast and replenish the vast majority of our store products through sophisticated inventory management systems and utilize our network of distribution centers to serve both our stores' and customers' needs. Our supply chain includes multiple distribution center platforms in the U.S., Canada, and Mexico tailored to meet the needs of our stores and customers based on types of products, location, transportation, and delivery requirements. These platforms include rapid deployment centers, stocking distribution centers, bulk distribution centers, and direct fulfillment centers, among others. As part of the expansion of our supply chain, we have invested to further automate and mechanize our rapid deployment center network to drive efficiency and faster movement of product.
We are also continuing to expand our fulfillment network, investing in a significant number of new fulfillment facilities to drive speed and reliability of delivery for our customers and to help us ultimately meet our goal of reaching 90% of the U.S. population with same or next day delivery for extended home improvement product offerings, including big and bulky products. These facilities include omni-channel fulfillment centers, which deliver product directly to customers, and market delivery operations, which function as local hubs to consolidate freight for dispatch to customers for the final mile of delivery, with a focus on appliances. In fiscal 2022, we realized our goal to control more of our appliance delivery end-to-end and began managing all of our appliance delivery volume through our market delivery operations. We have also added flatbed distribution centers, which handle large items like lumber and building materials that are transported on flatbed trucks. As of the end of fiscal 2022, we have opened a number of additional fulfillment facilities, and we will continue to build out our fulfillment network to support our business. Our network is designed to create a competitive advantage with unique, industry-leading capabilities for home improvement needs for both Pros and consumers.
In addition to our distribution and fulfillment centers, we leverage our stores as a network of convenient customer pickup, return, and delivery fulfillment locations. Our premium real estate footprint provides a distinct structural and competitive advantage. For customers who shop online and wish to pick up or return merchandise at, or have merchandise delivered from, our stores, we have implemented four interconnected retail programs: BOSS, BOPIS, BODFS, and BORIS. We also provide curbside pickup to complement our BOPIS offerings, in addition to the self-service lockers at the front entrance of many of our stores. We also offer express car and van delivery service that covers over 80% of the U.S. population. For fiscal 2022, approximately 50% of our U.S. online orders were fulfilled through a store. We also continue to focus on developing new capabilities to improve both efficiency and customer experience in our store delivery program. Our strategic intent is to have a portfolio of efficient, timely and reliable sources and methods of delivery to choose from, optimizing order fulfillment and delivery based on customer needs, inventory locations and available transportation options.

CORPORATE RESPONSIBILITY AND HUMAN CAPITAL MANAGEMENT
We view environmental, social and governance matters through the lens of our business, with an understanding that if we support our associates, our customers, our supplier partners, and the communities we serve, we also support our business and create long-term value for our shareholders. As a result, we believe that ESG is fundamentally embedded in our operations and culture. We organize our efforts around three pillars: (1) Focus on Our People, (2) Operate Sustainably, and (3) Strengthen Our Communities. Highlights of each of these pillars are set forth below. For further information on our three pillars and other ESG-related matters, see our annual ESG Report, available on our website at https://corporate.homedepot.com/responsibility.
Focus on Our People
Our culture and our associates provide intangible and hard-to-replicate competitive advantages, which have been key to helping us navigate challenging market conditions. Our associates are essential to providing the experience and service that our customers demand. To preserve and protect that customer experience, we focus on cultivating a compelling associate experience, which we believe supports our ability to attract and retain our associates. This includes investing in competitive wages and benefits while also providing the culture, tools, training and development opportunities that make working at The Home Depot an enjoyable and rewarding experience. These actions are the foundation of our key tenets of putting customers first and taking care of our associates.
Culture and Values.
The Home Depot has a strong commitment to ethics and integrity, and we are a values- and culture-centric business. Our commitment to our core values drives our approach to human capital management. Our culture is based on our servant leadership philosophy represented by the inverted pyramid, which puts primary importance on our customers and our associates by positioning them at the top, with senior management at the base in a support role. We bring our culture to life through our core values, which serve as the foundation of our business and as the guiding principles behind the decisions we make every day.

Our values also guide our efforts to create an environment that will help us attract and retain skilled associates in the competitive marketplace for talent. We empower our associates to deliver a superior customer experience by living our values, and we position our associates to embody our core values by integrating the importance of our culture into ongoing development programs, performance management practices, and rewards programs. Leaders participate in programs designed to build and strengthen our culture, such as training on leadership skills, cross-functional collaboration, inclusiveness, and associate engagement, and all associates receive annual training on unconscious bias. Our core values are at the root of our human capital management programs.

HD.quantitative_report:
## Quantitative Report: Home Depot (HD)
**Decision Date: 2024-07-01 | Latest Filing: 2024-04-28 (Q1 FY2024)**

---

### 1. Revenue, Earnings, Margin & Balance-Sheet Trajectory

**Revenue:** TTM revenue of $151.83B. Latest Q1 revenue of $36.42B is -2.3% YoY (vs. Q1 FY2023: $37.26B) but +4.7% QoQ. Revenue has contracted from a Q2 FY2022 peak of $43.79B, with the trailing four-quarter path: $43.79B → $38.87B → $35.83B → $37.26B → $42.92B → $37.71B → $34.79B → $36.42B. This confirms a sustained negative revenue trend.

**Earnings:** Latest Q1 EPS of $3.63 is -5.0% YoY (vs. $3.82) and +28.3% QoQ (vs. $2.83). TTM net income is $14.87B. EPS has declined from a Q2 FY2022 peak of $5.05. The YoY decline accelerates from revenue (-2.3%) to EPS (-5.0%), indicating operating deleverage.

**Margins (from income statements):**
| Period | Gross Margin | Operating Margin |
|---|---|---|
| Q1 FY2024 | 34.1% ($12.43B/$36.42B) | 13.9% ($5.08B/$36.42B) |
| Q2 FY2022 (peak) | 33.1% ($14.48B/$43.79B) | 16.5% ($7.21B/$43.79B) |

Gross margin is stable-to-improving (33.1% → 34.1%), but operating margin has contracted ~260bps, indicating SG&A/operating cost pressure. Net margin (per valuation data) is 9.8%, down from 10.8% a year prior.

**Balance Sheet:** Total equity is $1.82B on $79.23B in assets, yielding a total-liabilities-to-equity ratio of 42.5x ($77.41B / $1.82B). Total debt (as of 2024-01-28) is $42.15B. Equity has been volatile and thin: $237M (Jul 2022) → $362M (Apr 2023) → $1.82B (Apr 2024). **Cash flow data is unavailable.**

---

### 2. Earnings Quality & Balance-Sheet Resilience

**Earnings Quality:** Without cash flow statements, free cash flow conversion cannot be directly verified. However, earnings quality signals are mixed: (a) the YoY decline in earnings (-5.0%) outpaces the revenue decline (-2.3%), suggesting margin pressure rather than cost discipline; (b) operating income of $5.08B on revenue of $36.42B yields a 13.9% operating margin, compressing from 16.5% at the revenue peak, indicating operating leverage works against the company in a down-revenue environment.

**Balance-Sheet Resilience:** This is the most significant quantitative concern. Total equity of $1.82B is minimal relative to the $79.23B asset base. The debt-to-equity ratio of 23.2x ($42.15B / $1.82B) is extreme. The equity base has swung from $237M to $1.82B over 8 quarters, suggesting significant retained-earnings volatility or share buyback drain. Resilience depends entirely on consistent operating cash flow generation—data that is not available here. The thin equity cushion provides virtually no margin of safety in a prolonged downturn.

---

### 3. Valuation vs. Own History

**Current multiples (2024-06-28):**
- P/E diluted: 22.965x
- P/B: 187.63x (distorted by thin equity)
- P/S: 2.249x
- Earnings yield: 4.4%
- ROE: 8.17%

**12-month P/E range: 17.1x – 26.2x.** Current P/E of 22.965x sits in the 60th percentile of this range—above median, not cheap.

**ROE trajectory is alarming:** 46.3% (Jul 2023) → 12.2% (Sep 2023) → 8.2% (latest). This collapse from 46.3% to 8.17%—a 38-percentage-point decline in one year—reflects either a one-time prior-period distortion or a genuine impairment of capital efficiency. Either way, current ROE of 8.17% does not justify a P/B of 187.6x.

Net margin has compressed from 10.8% to 9.8% over the trailing window. The valuation assigns a premium multiple to a business exhibiting declining margins, declining ROE, and declining revenue.

---

### 4. Price Level & Returns vs. Fundamentals

**Price (2024-06-28):** $344.24. 52-week range: $274.26 – $396.87. Current price sits 13.3% below the 52-week high.

**Return context:**
| Period | Price Change | Fundamentals |
|---|---|---|
| Q2 2024 (latest ~63d) | -10.3% ($383.60 → $344.24) | Q1 YoY revenue -2.3%, EPS -5.0% |
| Q1 2024 (~63d) | +10.1% ($348.53 → $383.60) | Q4 YoY revenue -2.9%, EPS -14.8% |
| Q4 2023 (~63d) | +15.5% ($301.82 → $348.53) | Q3 YoY revenue -3.0%, EPS -10.1% |
| Q3 2023 (~63d) | -1.5% ($306.51 → $301.82) | Q2 YoY revenue -2.0%, EPS -7.9% |
| Annual (~252d) | +12.3% ($306.51 → $344.24) | TTM rev -2.6% vs. prior TTM |

The stock rose 12.3% annually while TTM revenue contracted ~2.6% YoY and margins compressed. The most recent quarter saw a -10.3% price correction, partially aligning price with deteriorating fundamentals, but the annual return still significantly exceeds the fundamental trajectory.

---

### 5. Strongest Quantitative Upside & Downside

**Upside:**
- QoQ momentum in the latest quarter: revenue +4.7%, EPS +28.3% (from $2.83 to $3.63), suggesting stabilization or a cyclical trough.
- Gross margin stability/improvement (33.1% → 34.1%) indicates pricing power and cost of goods discipline remain intact.
- At the low end of the P/E range (17.1x) applied to TTM EPS of ~$14.94, a price target of ~$255 is possible in a downside scenario; at 23x current earnings, the market appears to be pricing a stabilization.
- Annual return of +12.3% confirms long-term holder resilience.

**Downside:**
- Revenue is in a confirmed multi-quarter downtrend: $43.79B (Q2 FY2022) → $36.42B (Q1 FY2024), a -16.8% decline from peak.
- ROE collapse from 46.3% to 8.17% with P/B of 187.6x represents a severe capital-efficiency mismatch.
- Equity base of $1.82B vs. $42.15B in debt offers no balance-sheet buffer; a 5% decline in asset values would eliminate more than 2x total equity.
- Cash flow is unverified—dividend sustainability (implied by thin equity and high leverage) cannot be confirmed.
- Operating margin contraction (16.5% → 13.9%) during a revenue decline indicates negative operating leverage that could accelerate further.

---

### 6. Provisional Quantitative Risk/Reward Conclusion

**Risk/reward is unfavorable at current levels.** The stock trades at 23.0x trailing earnings (above the 12-month median of ~21x) against a backdrop of declining revenue (-2.3% YoY), declining margins (operating margin 13.9% vs. 16.5% peak), collapsing ROE (8.17% vs. 46.3%), and an extraordinarily thin equity cushion ($1.82B on $79.23B assets). The -10.3% quarterly price decline is directionally appropriate but insufficient to offset the cumulative fundamental deterioration. The P/B of 187.6x on 8.17% ROE represents a valuation structure that requires either a rapid ROE recovery or sustained earnings stability—neither of which is evidenced in the available trajectory data. The absence of cash-flow data removes a critical verification layer for balance-sheet sustainability. Quantitative evidence supports a cautious stance until revenue stabilizes, operating margins re-expand, or the valuation compresses to levels (sub-18x) that more adequately compensate for the observed risks.
HD.qualitative_report:
**Qualitative Report: Home Depot (HD) | Decision Date: 2024-07-01**

**1. Business/Franchise Read**
Home Depot is the world's largest home improvement retailer, operating 2,322 stores (as of fiscal 2022) across the U.S., Canada, and Mexico. Its strategy centers on three goals: providing the best customer experience, being the low-cost provider, and being the most efficient capital allocator in the sector. The company serves two primary customer segments: DIY homeowners and Professional customers ("Pros"), including contractors and renovators. A key strategic focus is deepening engagement with Pros through initiatives like the Pro Xtra loyalty program, dedicated sales forces, and expanded supply chain capabilities to service their planned purchase needs. The acquisition of HD Supply enhanced its MRO (Maintenance, Repair, and Operations) offerings. The business model relies on an "interconnected experience," blending its large physical store footprint with digital and mobile capabilities, supported by investments in supply chain automation and fulfillment (e.g., market delivery operations, flatbed distribution centers). Capital allocation prioritizes reinvestment for growth, followed by dividends and share repurchases.

**2. Recent Developments (Dated)**
*   **Competition & Market Sentiment (2024-06-25):** HD stock declined after Pool Corp. issued a negative preannouncement, highlighting sensitivity to the broader housing and home improvement market. Consumer Confidence Index data from June 2024 showed a dip to 100.4, indicating persistent consumer economic concerns.
*   **Strategic Growth Catalyst (2024-06-02):** The forthcoming acquisition of SRS Distribution is positioned as a complement to its strategy of locking in professional customers.
*   **Analyst Sentiment (2024-06-26):** Analysis of 22 analysts over the prior three months revealed diverse perspectives, with ratings ranging from "Bullish" (9 analysts) to "Bearish" (4 analysts).
*   **Regulatory/Legal Action (2024-06-28 & 2024-06-30):** The Washington State Department of Ecology fined Home Depot $1.6 million for selling products containing hydrofluorocarbons (HFCs) restricted under state law. The company has 30 days to appeal.
*   **Valuation Assessment (2024-05-22):** Morningstar rated HD stock with 2 stars ("overvalued"), setting a long-term fair value estimate of $265 per share, well below its market price at that time.

**3. Material Qualitative Risks & Offsets**
*   **Risks:**
    *   **Housing Market & Macro Sensitivity:** The business is highly cyclical. The negative reaction to Pool Corp.'s preannouncement (2024-06-25) and declining consumer confidence (2024-06-25) underscore vulnerability to housing activity and discretionary spending slowdowns.
    *   **Regulatory & Legal:** The $1.6 million fine in Washington state for HFC violations (2024-06-28) and ongoing lawsuits alleging misleading "fake reference" pricing (2024-05-13, 2024-05-20) pose reputational, financial, and operational compliance risks.
    *   **Valuation:** Independent analysis suggests the stock may be overvalued relative to fundamental estimates (2024-05-22), creating potential downside risk if growth falters.
*   **Offsets:**
    *   **Pro Customer Focus:** The large, fragmented $500 billion pro contractor market is projected to grow at 4.4% annually (2024-06-24), representing a significant growth runway. HD's strategic investments (loyalty, sales force, supply chain, SRS acquisition) are designed to capture this demand.
    *   **Operational Scale & Strategy:** Its dominant scale, low-cost provider goal, and capital-efficient model provide a durable competitive moat. Ongoing investments in the interconnected shopping experience and supply chain enhance efficiency and customer retention.
    *   **Brand Strength:** As the world's largest home improvement retailer with highly recognized brands (e.g., Husky, HDX), it possesses significant intangible value.

**4. Concise Qualitative Assessment**
**Neutral-to-Slightly-Negative.** Home Depot's business franchise is robust, anchored by market leadership, a strong focus on the growing professional segment, and strategic investments in interconnected retail and supply chain. However, the recent narrative is tempered by clear headwinds. Evidence points to sensitivity to the housing cycle and weakening consumer sentiment, alongside tangible legal and regulatory fines that introduce risk. While the long-term strategy targeting the pro market is a credible offset, the current environment presents near-term challenges to growth and profitability, contributing to a cautious near-term outlook.

corrections.issue: Quant report states 'total debt (as of 2024-01-28) is $42.15B' and uses this figure to compute debt-to-equity of 23.2x, but the latest balance sheet date is 2024-04-28 where total_debt is not provided in the raw pack. The $42.15B figure is from the prior quarter (2024-01-28). The report should note this mismatch rather than presenting the ratio as if both figures are from the same date.
corrections.required_action: Clarify that the debt-to-equity ratio uses debt from 2024-01-28 and equity from 2024-04-28, which may overstate or understate the true current leverage.

corrections.issue: Quant report states 'ROE trajectory is alarming: 46.3% (Jul 2023) → 12.2% (Sep 2023) → 8.2% (latest). This collapse from 46.3% to 8.17%—a 38-percentage-point decline in one year—reflects either a one-time prior-period distortion or a genuine impairment of capital efficiency.' The raw valuation data shows ROE=46.262 on 2023-07-03 and 2023-08-04, then ROE=12.16 on 2023-09-06. This is a ~34pp drop in one month, which is almost certainly an artifact of the extremely thin equity base (e.g., equity was $237M in Jul 2022, $362M in Apr 2023). The report correctly flags the distortion possibility but does not adequately explain that with equity this thin, ROE is essentially meaningless as a valuation signal. The P/B of 187.6x is similarly distorted and should be explicitly downweighted rather than treated as a primary valuation concern.
corrections.required_action: Add a note that P/B and ROE are unreliable metrics given the sub-$2B equity base on a $79B asset company, and that P/E and P/S are more meaningful valuation anchors for this analysis.

corrections.issue: Qual report states '2,322 stores (as of fiscal 2022)' sourced from the 10-K. The 10-K filing is dated 2023-03-15 with period_end 2023-01-29, and the raw evidence notes it is 474 days before the decision date. The qual report does not flag that this store count may be stale. This is a minor point but worth noting for completeness.
corrections.required_action: No action required; this is a minor staleness note consistent with the data available.

unsupported_claims: Quant report: 'The equity base has swung from $237M to $1.82B over 8 quarters, suggesting significant retained-earnings volatility or share buyback drain.' The raw pack provides no cash flow or share repurchase data to support the 'share buyback drain' attribution. The swing is stated in the raw data but the cause is speculative and not evidenced in the supplied packs.

Quant report: 'a 5% decline in asset values would eliminate more than 2x total equity.' This arithmetic claim is not directly verifiable from the raw pack without knowing the composition of assets (e.g., how much is goodwill, inventory, PP&E). A 5% decline in total assets ($79.23B × 5% = $3.96B) would indeed exceed the $1.82B equity, but the 'more than 2x' framing ($3.96B / $1.82B ≈ 2.2x) is approximately correct. However, the claim implicitly assumes a uniform asset write-down scenario that is not supported by any balance-sheet detail in the raw pack.

Qual report: 'the $500 billion pro contractor market is projected to grow at 4.4% annually (2024-06-24)' — this figure is sourced from a Bloomberg web context article and is presented as fact. The raw evidence does include this in the web context [opportunity] block, so it is within the evidence boundary, but the report should note this is a third-party industry estimate, not Home Depot's own projection.

pm_decision_obj.executive_summary: Home Depot's best-in-class franchise and pro-customer strategy provide long-term resilience, but the stock trades at 23x trailing earnings amid confirmed revenue decline (-2.3% YoY), operating margin compression (13.9% vs. 16.5% peak), and an extraordinarily thin equity cushion ($1.82B on $79B assets). The recent -10.3% quarterly correction partially re-aligns price with fundamentals, but not enough to create a compelling entry point given the cyclical headwinds.
pm_decision_obj.investment_thesis: Home Depot's best-in-class franchise and pro-customer strategy provide long-term resilience, but the stock trades at 23x trailing earnings amid confirmed revenue decline (-2.3% YoY), operating margin compression (13.9% vs. 16.5% peak), and an extraordinarily thin equity cushion ($1.82B on $79B assets). The recent -10.3% quarterly correction partially re-aligns price with fundamentals, but not enough to create a compelling entry point given the cyclical headwinds.
key_factors: P/E of 23.0x sits in the 60th percentile of its 12-month range (17.1x-26.2x) and above the ~21x median, reflecting premium valuation on deteriorating fundamentals
Q1 FY2024 revenue declined -2.3% YoY to $36.42B and EPS fell -5.0% YoY to $3.63, with operating margin compressing to 13.9% from 16.5% at the revenue peak, confirming negative operating leverage
Balance sheet equity of $1.82B against $42.15B in debt (as of 2024-01-28) provides virtually no margin of safety; P/B and ROE metrics are distorted by the thin equity base and should be downweighted

Pro contractor market ($500B, growing 4.4% annually per third-party estimates) and the SRS Distribution acquisition provide credible long-term growth optionality that partially offsets near-term cyclical weakness
